pattern: Subtracting two numbers flips parity exactly when they differ in parity, so any element can be forced to either parity as long as some other element of the needed parity exists somewhere else in the array -- which, across the whole array, is always the case.
hints: A difference nums1[i] - nums1[j] is odd exactly when nums1[i] and nums1[j] have different parities, and even when they share a parity -- you never need the actual values, only the parities.
Aim to make every element odd: any element that is already odd can just use itself; any even element can borrow one of the (necessarily present) odd elements to flip its own contribution to odd.

Work out when that plan can fail -- it only fails if there is no odd element anywhere in the array, and in that situation every element is already even, so the array is uniform without changing anything.

edge_inputs.description: exactly one odd element among several evens
exactly one even element among several odds
mixed parities where the smallest value is even
values at the maximum allowed magnitude

stress.description: random distinct values across the full allowed range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    nums1 = rng.sample(range(1, 101), n)
    return [nums1]
